expectations.text: has-phases: Output contains between 4 and 8 clearly labeled phases
expectations.evidence: Output contains 7 clearly labeled phases: Phase 1 (Accept Invitation and Onboard), Phase 2 (Navigate Dashboard and Select Client), Phase 3 (Upload Client Documents), Phase 4 (Review Extracted Data), Phase 5 (Review Pre-Filled Tax Forms), Phase 6 (Submit to Tax Authority), Phase 7 (Track Filing Status and Follow Up).

expectations.text: has-happy-paths: Every phase has a 'Happy path' section with numbered steps
expectations.evidence: All 7 phases have a '**Happy path:**' section with numbered steps. Phase 1 has 4 steps, Phase 2 has 3, Phase 3 has 4, Phase 4 has 5, Phase 5 has 5, Phase 6 has 5, Phase 7 has 4.

expectations.text: has-exceptions: Every phase has an 'Exceptions' section with at least one exception
expectations.evidence: All 7 phases have an '**Exceptions:**' section. Phase 1 has 2, Phase 2 has 2, Phase 3 has 3, Phase 4 has 3, Phase 5 has 3, Phase 6 has 3, Phase 7 has 3 exceptions.

expectations.text: has-mermaid: Output contains a valid Mermaid diagram (```mermaid code block with graph TD or graph LR)
expectations.evidence: Output contains a ```mermaid code block starting with 'graph TD' at line 129, with a comprehensive flowchart covering all phases.

expectations.text: persona-focus: Journey focuses on the accountant persona, not the firm admin
expectations.evidence: The overview states 'This journey maps the projected experience of an accountant user.' All steps are from the accountant's perspective (receives invitation, uploads documents, reviews data, submits filing). The firm admin is only mentioned tangentially (e.g., 'firm admin sends invitation', 'admin approval required').

expectations.text: user-facing-steps: Steps describe what the user sees/does, not system internals
expectations.evidence: Steps consistently describe user actions and what they see: 'Accountant receives an email invitation', 'Accountant clicks the link and lands on a registration page', 'Accountant sees a structured summary of extracted fields', 'Platform shows a final confirmation summary'. System processing is described from the user's perspective (e.g., 'Platform validates file types and sizes, shows upload progress').

All exceptions provide resolution paths: expired link -> 'button to request a new invitation'; unsupported file format -> 'message listing accepted formats'; extraction fails -> 'form for the accountant to input data by hand'; tax authority rejects -> 'links the accountant back to the relevant form fields to correct and resubmit'; API unavailable -> 'automatically retries; accountant receives a notification when submission succeeds'.

expectations.text: has-overview: Output starts with an Overview section
expectations.evidence: The document starts with '## Overview' at line 3, providing a summary of the journey: 'This journey maps the projected experience of an accountant user within a B2B SaaS platform that automates client tax filing for small accounting firms.'
